title: How Love and Oneness Unlock Million-Dollar Business Success with Dave Lundgren
guestBio: Dave Lundgren is a transformational coach, real estate investor, and bestselling author of 'Loveness' who has collaborated with Tony Robbins, Dean Graziosi, and other industry leaders to build companies generating over a billion dollars in sales. A former U.S. Army chaplain, Dave teaches a philosophy beyond mindset, helping entrepreneurs connect to transformation through love, consciousness, and the balance of masculine and feminine energies.
excerpt: What if the secret to breakthrough business success isn't another strategy or mindset hack, but understanding two universal laws that both science and spirituality confirm? Dave Lundgren reveals how Love and Oneness became the foundation for building billion-dollar companies alongside Tony Robbins and Dean Graziosi.
metaDescription: Dave Lundgren shares how Love and Oneness universal laws drive billion-dollar business success, plus insights on AI, real estate, and conscious leadership.
keyTakeaways: Love and Oneness are universal laws backed by both quantum physics and spirituality that drive true business success.
Top entrepreneurs tap into feminine energy for visionary breakthroughs while using masculine energy for execution and structure.
Women need approximately 20% masculine energy to effectively scale their businesses and lead with authority.
Authenticity is your most powerful marketing tool because it allows you to genuinely connect with your ideal audience.
Real estate investing offers four returns on every dollar invested, making it superior to traditional stock market investing.
AI should be used as a tool to free you to be more human, not replace human consciousness and connection.
The measure of mastery in leadership is how quickly you can recover from setbacks and challenges.

pullQuotes.text: The false sense of separation is the root cause of most human suffering in business and life.
Top entrepreneurs like Tony Robbins tap into feminine energy for visionary breakthroughs.
Your pain and challenges become your greatest gifts when transformed.

timestamps.label: From Army Chaplain to Business Consultant
The Dark Night of the Ego
Masculine and Feminine Energy in Business
AI and the Fourth Industrial Revolution
Real Estate Investing at Every Level

content:
What if everything you've been taught about business success is missing the most fundamental piece? In this transformative episode of The Manage Her, host Aimee Rickabus sits down with Dave Lundgren, a man whose journey from Army chaplain to billion-dollar business consultant reveals profound truths about leadership, wealth creation, and human consciousness.

Dave's story begins in an unexpected place—military ministry. But it was his dark night of the ego that catalyzed everything. This wasn't just a spiritual awakening; it was a complete dismantling of everything he thought he knew about success, relationships, and life itself. What emerged from that transformation was a deep understanding of two universal laws that both quantum physics and ancient wisdom traditions confirm: Love and Oneness.

The Science Behind Spiritual Success

You might be thinking, "This sounds too woo-woo for business." But here's what makes Dave's approach revolutionary—he bridges the gap between spirituality and hard business results. Having worked alongside titans like Tony Robbins, Dean Graziosi, Mark Victor Hansen, and Forbes Riley, Dave has helped build companies generating over a billion dollars in sales. His secret? Understanding that the false sense of separation is the root cause of most human suffering, including the struggles you face in your business.

When you operate from Love and Oneness, you're not just changing your mindset—you're aligning with universal principles that govern everything from quantum mechanics to human psychology. This isn't about positive thinking; it's about tapping into the fundamental forces that create reality itself.

The Energy Game That Changes Everything

One of the most practical revelations Dave shares is the critical balance between masculine and feminine energy in business. You've probably been told to "lean in" or "boss up," but what if the real secret is more nuanced? Dave explains that top entrepreneurs like Tony Robbins understand this intuitively—they tap into feminine energy for visionary breakthroughs and innovation, then employ masculine energy for execution and structure.

For women entrepreneurs, this balance is especially crucial. Dave suggests that while you naturally operate from feminine energy (intuition, creativity, flow), you need approximately 20% masculine energy to effectively scale your business and lead with authority. This isn't about becoming someone you're not; it's about accessing all parts of yourself to create unstoppable momentum.

Think about the times when your business felt effortless and expansive—you were likely in feminine flow. Now consider the moments when you struggled to make decisions or execute consistently—you may have been lacking that grounding masculine structure. The magic happens when you consciously blend both energies.

Authenticity: Your Secret Marketing Weapon

In a world obsessed with funnels, frameworks, and formulas, Dave reveals that your most powerful marketing tool is something you already possess: authenticity. But this isn't surface-level "be yourself" advice. True authenticity requires shadow work—embracing and integrating the parts of yourself you've tried to hide or reject.

When you stop trying to be perfect and start being real, something miraculous happens. You begin to attract your ideal clients not through manipulation or clever copy, but through genuine resonance. Your struggles become relatable, your victories become inspiring, and your message becomes magnetic because it's rooted in truth.

Dave emphasizes that marketing is fundamentally about entering the conversation already happening in the marketplace. When you're authentic, you don't have to guess what that conversation sounds like—you're already part of it because you've lived it.

AI and the Future of Human Leadership

As we navigate the fourth industrial revolution, many entrepreneurs are wondering: Will AI replace what we do? Dave's perspective is both reassuring and challenging. AI should be a tool that frees you to be more human, not less. While artificial intelligence can handle data processing, pattern recognition, and even content creation, it can never replicate human consciousness, intuition, and the ability to truly connect with another person's experience.

This means your value as a leader isn't in competing with AI, but in doubling down on what makes you irreplaceably human. Your empathy, your ability to hold space for others' transformation, your intuitive leaps—these are the qualities that will become more valuable, not less, as technology advances.

Real Estate: The Wealth Vehicle You Can't Ignore

Beyond the philosophical insights, Dave delivers practical wealth-building wisdom through his extensive real estate investing experience. Unlike stocks, which offer only one potential return through appreciation, real estate provides four distinct returns on every dollar invested: cash flow, appreciation, tax benefits, and loan paydown. This isn't just theory—Dave has built substantial wealth through strategic real estate investments at every level of his financial journey.

The key insight? You don't need massive capital to start. You need education, strategy, and the willingness to take calculated risks. Real estate becomes a vehicle not just for financial freedom, but for funding your bigger mission and creating the lifestyle that supports your highest contribution.

The Four Pillars That Scale Any Business

Whether you're just starting out or looking to break through your current plateau, Dave shares the fundamental sequence that every successful business must master: Marketing leads to Sales, which funds Fulfillment, which generates Cashflow. This isn't just a business model—it's a systematic approach to creating sustainable wealth while serving others.

The revolutionary insight? Sell first, build later. Too many entrepreneurs get stuck in perfectionist mode, creating products or services before proving market demand. Dave advocates for entering the conversation in the marketplace first, understanding what people actually want, then creating solutions that genuinely serve those needs.

Leading from Love in a Chaotic World

As you navigate the complexities of modern leadership, Dave's message is both simple and profound: your greatest challenges become your greatest gifts when transformed through love and consciousness. The setbacks, the failures, the moments when everything falls apart—these aren't obstacles to your success. They're the raw materials for your transformation and, ultimately, your unique contribution to the world.

The measure of mastery isn't avoiding difficulties; it's how quickly you recover from a gut punch and transform that experience into wisdom that serves others. When you lead from this place of integrated experience, you don't just build a business—you create a legacy of transformation that ripples through every life you touch.

This conversation with Dave Lundgren isn't just about business strategy or personal development. It's about remembering who you really are beneath all the roles, responsibilities, and expectations—and leading from that place of authentic power. Because when you align your business with Love and Oneness, you don't just create wealth; you create a life and legacy that matter.

quiz.title: What's Your Business Energy Archetype?
quiz.description: Discover how you naturally balance intuition and action to create success. Based on Dave Lundgren's insights about feminine and masculine energy in leadership.

questions.question: When you're facing a major business decision, what's your natural first step?

options.text: I get quiet and tune into what feels aligned with my vision
I balance my gut feeling with data and practical considerations
I analyze the data, create a pros and cons list, and map out potential outcomes
I consider how this decision will impact everyone involved and align with my values

questions.question: How do you typically approach networking events or business meetings?

options.text: I'm drawn to conversations about big ideas and future possibilities
I enjoy both brainstorming sessions and tactical planning discussions
I prefer focused conversations about strategies, systems, and measurable outcomes
I focus on building genuine relationships and understanding people's deeper motivations

questions.question: When your business hits a challenging period, your natural response is to:

options.text: Step back and reimagine what's possible, trusting that clarity will come
Assess what's working and what isn't, then adjust both strategy and mindset
Create an action plan with clear steps and deadlines to work through it systematically
Reconnect with your why and ensure you're still serving your purpose authentically

questions.question: Your leadership style is best described as:

options.text: Inspiring others with a compelling vision of what could be
Guiding teams through both creative exploration and practical execution
Setting clear expectations and providing the structure for others to succeed
Creating an environment where people feel valued, heard, and empowered to contribute

questions.question: When it comes to marketing your business, you're most comfortable:

options.text: Sharing your big dreams and the transformation you want to create in the world
Combining your personal story with concrete results and social proof
Focusing on your track record, systems, and the specific results you deliver
Being completely authentic about your journey and connecting heart-to-heart with your audience

questions.question: Your biggest business growth typically happens when you:

options.text: Follow a sudden insight or intuitive hit that opens up new possibilities
Combine a creative breakthrough with disciplined execution over time
Implement proven systems and strategies with consistent, focused effort
Align more deeply with your purpose and attract the right people and opportunities

results.title: The Intuitive Visionary
results.description: You're naturally gifted at tapping into big-picture ideas and seeing possibilities others miss. Your feminine energy flows freely, giving you incredible creative and visionary power. However, you might sometimes struggle with execution and structure. This episode will show you exactly how to add that crucial 20% masculine energy to scale your dreams into reality without losing your intuitive magic.

results.title: The Balanced Catalyst
results.description: You have a natural ability to flow between visionary thinking and practical execution, making you a powerful force in business. You understand both the importance of connection and the need for structure. You're already living what Dave teaches about balancing energies. This episode will help you refine this gift even further and understand the spiritual principles behind your natural success pattern.

results.title: The Strategic Builder
results.description: Your strength lies in creating systems, executing plans, and building solid foundations. You excel at the masculine energy of business but might sometimes feel disconnected from your intuitive side. You're incredibly reliable and results-driven. Dave's insights about Love and Oneness will help you tap into the feminine visionary energy that can take your already impressive execution skills to the next level.

results.title: The Conscious Connector
results.description: You naturally understand that business is about relationships and authentic connection. You lead with love and genuinely care about the impact you make. Your challenge might be claiming your authority and making the tough decisions leadership requires. This episode will validate your heart-centered approach while showing you how to step into your full power as a leader without compromising your values.

transcript: We've been in the moment for too long Welcome back to The Manager, the show where we turn invisible work into visible leadership and explore what it really means to lead with love, vision, and truth. Today, I'm joined by Dave Lundgren, a transformational coach, real estate investor, and bestselling author of Loveness. With more than 25 years of leadership in real estate, and educational coaching, Dave's career began as a U.S. Army chaplain before transitioning into the world of business and investment. He's collaborated with some of the most influential names in personal development and business, including Tony Robbins, Dean DeGrazio, Mark Victor Hansen, and Forbes Riley, helping build and lead companies that have generated over a billion dollars in sales. Beyond his impressive credentials, Dave's true mission is deeply spiritual after what he calls his dark night of the ego. He began teaching a new philosophy beyond mindset, helping others connect to the root cause of transformation and success through love, consciousness, and surrender. Today, we're talking about the forbidden conversations, money, sex, and power, and how love, energy, and frequency are the true forces behind creation. Also, we'll explore how women are rising in this new age of AI, leadership, and spiritual intelligence. So grab your notebook or your glass of wine and let's dive in. I guess let's get into your journey from army chaplain to real estate executive. This isn't a typical one. What inspired you in that transition? What lessons about leadership carried over from your time in ministry? All right, great question. So what inspired me was honestly a little bit of desperation and a little bit of anger. I was stationed at Fort Leavenworth. That was my last station as a chaplain. I was going through a divorce and just the pain of that divorce, which I wasn't expecting or wanting, as I was making that transition, I met a gentleman who was one of the godfathers really of high ticket coaching. So you hear about all these real estate educational companies and such. So I met this gentleman. He's like, hey, I can help you make good money selling real estate coaching. And I was like, point me in that direction because I was fueled by just a little bit of anger, honestly, and just like I'm going to create a new life for myself. So I was really moving away from anything to do with spirituality and just I want to make some money. why I think it worked for me because I was really good at consulting people and whether I want to make money in real estate, I want to lose weight, I want to fix my marriage. The sales process they taught me was really, hey, you're here and you want to be here. How do you fill that gap in? So I became really good at that conversation. And from that, I was able to do well. And then I met Dean Graciosi, who you'd mentioned in the intro and really doing the infomercials with him just opened up all sorts of doors from there. So that's the very quick story on the transition. That's amazing. I had no idea when I met you, I knew we just had this connection with real estate, you know, we both real estate investors. That's like my first love in business was, I mean, I'd absolutely, you know, cut my first tooth on real estate deals. And, uh, so we had that in common and I, just to learn all of this, your interesting history in, uh, your time in ministry and all this stuff. I had no idea. I was just glancing through your book and you actually grew up Mormon. I did born and raised in Utah. Yeah. Wow. And went through that transition. And yeah, I talk about in that book a little bit. I'm more focused and deeply interested in my own spiritual walk, but I'm not necessarily a fan of organized religion per se. But yeah, I was born and raised in Utah, Mormon, went on the mission, all that kind of stuff. I had no idea. I was like, oh, wow. Then to find yourself as a chaplain in the army, I was like, wow, you've had quite the journey. You've described loveness as where science and spirituality converge in the truth. And I love this. What was the inspiration behind that idea? And what do you want readers to feel when they finish your book? Oh, that's a great question. What do I want them to feel? I want them to feel that love. When I say love, capital L, agape, Christlike love, whatever you want to call that. That's what I want them to feel. I want them to feel that. That which created them, which to me is the perfect source of love. And so that ties into what inspired and what is the book about. I found myself going through my dark night of the ego. And despite having... got my master's degree in family therapy, been around the coaching space, helped a lot of people with their businesses and all sorts of stuff, worked for Tony Robbins. I found myself going through a dark night of the ego and I'm like, okay, how did I find myself here? And really for the first time in my life, I truly surrendered and asked for help. And in that transition, as I go deep, I found that what you talked about in that Beyond Mindset, which is really my experience and observation is that most psychology and self-help and religion and personal development really is just focused on symptoms. And I believe therein lies a lot of the challenges, which the book talks about as well. If we could, as simple as it sounds, root ourselves in love and oneness, I believe it's a very different world. I'm with you right there. I get it. I totally get it.
